g.text: struct Pipe<X: AudioNode, Y: AudioNode<Inputs = X::Outputs>>
fn constant<X: ConstantFrame<Sample = f32>>(x: X) -> An<Constant<X::Size>>

ul.li: look how this library implement digital filter
look how this lib implement delay
and look how this lib use delay to make delay based effect like reverb

div.p:
entry point is coming from AudioNode.get_stereo() or AudioNode.get_mono(),
then it will calling tick() method AudioNode, for example if
program is like let c = constant(440.0) >> sine() c is Pipe and Pipe
is implement AudioNode (from this implementation impl<X, Y> Shr<An<Y>> for An<X>)
pipe will first call X then Y and producing new result.
constant(f) is producing Frame then output of constant (X) is piped to sine (Y)
